steps: Log in to your SendGrid account or sign up at https://signup.sendgrid.com
Click 'Create API Key' in the top-right corner
Enter a name for the API key
Select the permission level: 'Full Access', 'Restricted Access', or 'Billing Access'
If using Restricted Access, configure the specific permissions you need
Copy the API key displayed on screen — it starts with 'SG.' and is shown only once

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: Your SendGrid API key. Sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

notes: API key is shown only once — copy it before clicking 'Done'
Keys are 69 characters long and always start with 'SG.'
Use Restricted Access keys in production for least-privilege security
You must verify a sender identity (domain or single sender) before sending emails
Twilio acquired SendGrid; account management is unified under app.sendgrid.com

test_endpoint.description: List API keys to verify authentication is working
